parsed.mechanism: Treating an LLM's hidden state as a self-supervised model that trains on the test sequence induces a 'rupture-like' adaptation front whose update speed and energy distribution are regulated by structural irregularities in the input, transforming off-distribution tokens into high-frequency representational radiation analogous to off-fault damage.
parsed.transfer: The off-fault-damage principle that local heterogeneity reduces propagation speed and boosts high-frequency radiation transfers to test-time-training dynamics, where input irregularities slow hidden-state convergence and amplify high-frequency update components.
parsed.open_problem: Does input heterogeneity in a test-time-trained hidden state measurably slow its update-propagation speed while increasing high-frequency components of its representational trajectory?
parsed.niche_name: Rupture Dynamics of Test-Time-Training Hidden States
parsed.primary_quote: off-fault damage enhances high-frequency wave radiation above 1 Hz
